changes.value:
## Design

Bridge the gap between entity focus (which FocusScope was clicked) and command resolution (what commands the palette/keybindings can see).

### Registry

`EntityFocusProvider` gains a `Map<string, CommandScope>` ref (not state — no re-renders on register/deregister). Each FocusScope registers `(moniker, scope)` on mount via `useEffect`, deregisters on unmount.

The CommandScope already has a `.parent` pointer built by React's nested CommandScopeProviders, so registering the leaf scope gives access to the full chain.

### Focus resolution

New hook: `useFocusedScope(): CommandScope | null` — looks up `focusedMoniker` in the registry. Returns the focused entity's scope (with its full parent chain) or null if nothing is focused.

The palette and `KeybindingHandler` use `useFocusedScope()` to resolve commands from the focused entity's scope chain, falling back to the root AppShell scope when nothing is focused.

### "Am I focused?" hook

`useIsFocused(moniker): boolean` — returns true if:
- `focusedMoniker === moniker` (direct), OR
- The focused scope's ancestor chain includes this moniker (descendant focus)

This lets a column know "a card inside me is focused" and apply visual styles (e.g., subtle highlight). Cards know if a tag inside them is focused.

The ancestor check walks the registry: look up `focusedMoniker`, then walk `.parent` scopes checking if any FocusScope in the chain has this moniker.

### Unfocus

Click on empty board background → `setFocus(null)`. Add an onClick on the board backdrop area in BoardView.

## Subtasks

- [ ] Add `scopeRegistry: Map<string, CommandScope>` ref to EntityFocusProvider
- [ ] Add `registerScope(moniker, scope)` and `unregisterScope(moniker)` to the context value
- [ ] FocusScope calls registerScope on mount, unregisterScope on unmount (useEffect)
- [ ] Add `useFocusedScope()` hook that looks up focusedMoniker in the registry
- [ ] Add `useIsFocused(moniker)` hook — direct match OR ancestor chain match
- [ ] Wire KeybindingHandler to resolve from focused scope (fallback to root)
- [ ] Wire CommandPalette to show commands from focused scope
- [ ] Add unfocus: click on board background calls setFocus(null)
- [ ] Update data-focused attribute to use useIsFocused (so columns highlight when child is focused)
- [ ] Tests: registry lifecycle, useFocusedScope resolution, useIsFocused ancestor detection, unfocus on background click
- [ ] Run tests to verify no regressions
Scope registry: wire entity focus to command resolution

+## Mental Model

**Focus IS a CommandScope.** Clicking or navigating determines which CommandScope is focused. The moniker is just the address — the scope is the thing.

Everything flows from the focused scope:
- Palette shows commands from focused scope's chain
- Keybindings resolve from focused scope's chain
- Context menu collects from focused scope's chain
- Visual elements ask "is the focused scope me or a descendant of me?"

## Registry

`EntityFocusProvider` gains a `Map<string, CommandScope>` ref (not state — no re-renders on register/deregister). Each FocusScope registers `(moniker, scope)` on mount, deregisters on unmount.

The CommandScope already has a `.parent` pointer built by React's nesting. Registering the leaf gives access to the full chain. Add `moniker?: string` to CommandScope so ancestor walks can identify which scopes are FocusScope-created.

## Hooks

- `useFocusedScope(): CommandScope | null` — looks up focused moniker in registry, returns that scope (with full parent chain) or null
- `useIsFocused(moniker): boolean` — true if focused scope IS this moniker or has this moniker in its ancestor chain

## Wiring

KeyBindingHandler and CommandPalette use `useFocusedScope()` to resolve commands, falling back to root AppShell scope when null.

## Unfocus

Click on empty board background → `setFocus(null)` → focused scope is null → fall back to app scope.

## Subtasks

- [ ] Add `moniker?: string` to CommandScope interface
- [ ] FocusScope passes moniker when creating its CommandScope
- [ ] Add `scopeRegistry: Map<string, CommandScope>` ref to EntityFocusProvider
- [ ] Add `registerScope` / `unregisterScope` to context value
- [ ] FocusScope registers on mount, deregisters on unmount (useEffect)
- [ ] Add `useFocusedScope()` hook
- [ ] Add `useIsFocused(moniker)` hook with ancestor chain walk
- [ ] Wire KeybindingHandler to resolve from focused scope (fallback to root)
- [ ] Wire CommandPalette to show commands from focused scope
- [ ] Add unfocus: click on board background calls setFocus(null)
- [ ] Update data-focused to use useIsFocused (columns highlight when child focused)
- [ ] Tests: registry lifecycle, useFocusedScope, useIsFocused ancestor detection, unfocus
- [ ] Run tests to verify no regressions
\ No newline at end of file

 title: 'Scope registry: wire entity focus to command resolution'
 ---
 ## Mental Model

**Focus IS a CommandScope.** Clicking or navigating determines which CommandScope is focused. The moniker is just the address — the scope is the thing.

Everything flows from the focused scope:
- Palette shows commands from focused scope's chain
- Keybindings resolve from focused scope's chain
- Context menu collects from focused scope's chain
- Visual elements ask "is the focused scope me or a descendant of me?"

## Registry

`EntityFocusProvider` gains a `Map<string, CommandScope>` ref (not state — no re-renders on register/deregister). Each FocusScope registers `(moniker, scope)` on mount, deregisters on unmount.

The CommandScope already has a `.parent` pointer built by React's nesting. Registering the leaf gives access to the full chain. Add `moniker?: string` to CommandScope so ancestor walks can identify which scopes are FocusScope-created.

## Hooks

- `useFocusedScope(): CommandScope | null` — looks up focused moniker in registry, returns that scope (with full parent chain) or null
- `useIsFocused(moniker): boolean` — true if focused scope IS this moniker or has this moniker in its ancestor chain

## Wiring

KeyBindingHandler and CommandPalette use `useFocusedScope()` to resolve commands, falling back to root AppShell scope when null.

## Unfocus

Click on empty board background → `setFocus(null)` → focused scope is null → fall back to app scope.

## Subtasks

- [ ] Add `moniker?: string` to CommandScope interface
- [ ] FocusScope passes moniker when creating its CommandScope
- [ ] Add `scopeRegistry: Map<string, CommandScope>` ref to EntityFocusProvider
- [ ] Add `registerScope` / `unregisterScope` to context value
- [ ] FocusScope registers on mount, deregisters on unmount (useEffect)
- [ ] Add `useFocusedScope()` hook
- [ ] Add `useIsFocused(moniker)` hook with ancestor chain walk
- [ ] Wire KeybindingHandler to resolve from focused scope (fallback to root)
- [ ] Wire CommandPalette to show commands from focused scope
- [ ] Add unfocus: click on board background calls setFocus(null)
- [ ] Update data-focused to use useIsFocused (columns highlight when child focused)
- [ ] Tests: registry lifecycle, useFocusedScope, useIsFocused ancestor detection, unfocus
- [ ] Run tests to verify no regressions
\ No newline at end of file
